raw_consumer_statement: I submitted a dispute using Barclays website, for a purchase made through XXXX totaling {$2000.00}. I never received this purchase and the seller had specifically asked me to send a friends and family payment, after refunding my goods+service payment. I never got any information about this case outcome, until I called into Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX. I was told over the phone that XXXX claims that friends and family payments are a violation of their XXXX XXXX XXXX. I was told by Barclays representative to send over all my documents supporting my case, to have it reopened. I sent by mail AND fax. On Monday, XX/XX/XXXX, I called to make sure Barclays received it, just as the last agent had advised that I do. I was told yes, we got it on XX/XX/XXXX. Today I received a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX from Barclays stating that they were siding with the merchant and that " services were rendered. '' That is not true, and there is not a single shred of evidence to support that. I called again to billing disputes department XX/XX/XXXX. Here, I was told there was a mistake and my case with mishandled by a customer service agent who failed to " pend '' my case and have a manager review it. It was incorrectly closed out. They also counted my fax and mailed copy of the same review as a duplicate, and essentially said I could not re-open it. The agent dug a little further and found the mistake, also that there had only been XXXX letter of communication with me in regards to case results and said that would be grounds for them to re-open the case. I am sending the information he requested, but also opening a case here as it is evident that the case has already been mishandled. I was also given a case report number that was in their internal notes XXXX stating that the case was closed incorrectly without manager review.
